title: XEQT ETF Review 2026: iShares All Equity ETF vs VEQT
description: Complete 2026 review of XEQT (iShares Core Equity ETF). See performance vs VEQT, MER, asset allocation, underlying ETFs, and if it's the best ETF.

mainEntity.name: Is XEQT a good investment for 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, XEQT remains a strong choice for long-term investors seeking a globally diversified, 100% equity portfolio in a single ETF with a low management fee.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between XEQT and VEQT?

acceptedAnswer.text: Both are 100% equity ETFs, but XEQT has a slightly higher US allocation (approx. 45% vs 43%), lower Canadian allocation (25% vs 30%), and a slightly lower MER.

mainEntity.name: What is the MER of XEQT?

acceptedAnswer.text: XEQT has a Management Expense Ratio (MER) of 0.20%, which is very competitive for an all-in-one global equity portfolio.
Yes, XEQT pays distributions on a quarterly basis. The yield fluctuates based on the underlying global holdings.

mainEntity.name: What are the underlying ETFs in XEQT?

acceptedAnswer.text: XEQT is a fund of funds that holds four other iShares ETFs: ITOT (US equity), XIC (Canadian equity), XEF (International equity), and IEMG (Emerging Markets equity).
